heading: Definitions
label: 6511 Definitions

children.label: i
children.text: an interface community as defined in the notice entitled “Wildland Urban Interface Communities Within the Vicinity of Federal Lands That Are at High Risk From Wildfire” issued by the Secretary of Agriculture and the Secretary of the Interior in accordance with title IV of the Department of the Interior and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2001 () (66 Fed. Reg. 753, ); or114 Stat. 1009January 4, 2001

children.label: ii
children.text: a group of homes and other structures with basic infrastructure and services (such as utilities and collectively maintained transportation routes) within or adjacent to Federal land;

children.label: B
children.text: in which conditions are conducive to a large-scale wildland fire disturbance event; and

children.label: C
children.text: for which a significant threat to human life or property exists as a result of a wildland fire disturbance event.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: The term “authorized hazardous fuel reduction project” means the measures and methods described in the definition of “appropriate tools” contained in the glossary of the Implementation Plan, on Federal land described in and conducted under sections 6513 and 6514 of this title.section 6512(a) of this title

children.label: i
children.text: a natural or manmade change in fuel characteristics that affects fire behavior such that a fire can be more readily controlled (commonly known as a “fuel break”); and

children.label: ii
children.text: a natural or constructed barrier used to stop or check a fire or to provide a control line from which to work to stop or check a fire (commonly known as a “firebreak”).

children.label: A
children.text: is developed within the context of the collaborative agreements and the guidance established by the Wildland Fire Leadership Council and agreed to by the applicable local government, local fire department, and State agency responsible for forest management, in consultation with interested parties and the Federal land management agencies managing land in the vicinity of the at-risk community;

children.label: B
children.text: identifies and prioritizes areas for hazardous fuel reduction treatments and recommends the types and methods of treatment on Federal and non-Federal land that will protect 1 or more at-risk communities and essential infrastructure; and

children.label: C
children.text: recommends measures to reduce structural ignitability throughout the at-risk community.

children.label: A
children.text: fire regimes on the land have been moderately altered from historical ranges;

children.label: B
children.text: there exists a moderate risk of losing key ecosystem components from fire;

children.label: i
children.text: the size, frequency, intensity, or severity of fires; or

children.label: ii
children.text: landscape patterns; and

children.label: D
children.text: vegetation attributes have been moderately altered from the historical range of the attributes.

children.label: A
children.text: fire regimes on land have been significantly altered from historical ranges;

children.label: B
children.text: there exists a high risk of losing key ecosystem components from fire;

children.label: i
children.text: the size, frequency, intensity, or severity of fires; or

children.label: ii
children.text: landscape patterns; and

children.label: D
children.text: vegetation attributes have been significantly altered from the historical range of the attributes.

children.label: A
children.text: a calendar day; or

children.label: B
children.text: if a deadline imposed by this subchapter would expire on a nonbusiness day, the end of the next business day.

children.label: A
children.text: a decision notice (as that term is used in the Forest Service Handbook);

children.label: B
children.text: a decision record (as that term is used in the Bureau of Land Management Handbook); and

children.label: C
children.text: a record of decision (as that term is used in applicable regulations of the Council on Environmental Quality).

children.label: A
children.text: in which historically there have been low-severity fires with a frequency of 0 through 35 years; and

children.label: B
children.text: that is located primarily in low elevation forests of pine, oak, or pinyon juniper.

children.label: A
children.text: in which historically there are stand replacement severity fires with a frequency of 0 through 35 years; and

children.label: B
children.text: that is located primarily in low- to mid-elevation rangeland, grassland, or shrubland.

children.label: A
children.text: in which historically there are mixed severity fires with a frequency of 35 through 100 years; and

children.label: B
children.text: that is located primarily in forests of mixed conifer, dry Douglas fir, or wet Ponderosa pine.

children.heading: Implementation Plan
children.label: 11 Implementation Plan
children.text: The term “Implementation Plan” means the Implementation Plan for the Comprehensive Strategy for a Collaborative Approach for Reducing Wildland Fire Risks to Communities and the Environment, dated May 2002, developed pursuant to the conference report to accompany the Department of the Interior and Related Agencies Appropriations Act, 2001 (House Report No. 106–64) (and subsequent revisions).1See References in Text note below.1

children.heading: Municipal water supply system
children.label: 12 Municipal water supply system
children.text: The term “municipal water supply system” means the reservoirs, canals, ditches, flumes, laterals, pipes, pipelines, and other surface facilities and systems constructed or installed for the collection, impoundment, storage, transportation, or distribution of drinking water.

children.label: A
children.text: a land and resource management plan prepared for 1 or more units of land of the National Forest System described in under ; orsection 6502(1)(A) of this titlesection 1604 of this title

children.label: B
children.text: a land use plan prepared for 1 or more units of the public land described in under .section 6502(1)(B) of this titlesection 1712 of title 43

children.label: A
children.text: the Secretary of Agriculture, with respect to land of the National Forest System described in ; andsection 6502(1)(A) of this title

children.label: B
children.text: the Secretary of the Interior, with respect to public lands described in .section 6502(1)(B) of this title

children.label: A
children.text: a determination that a species is an endangered species or a threatened species under the Endangered Species Act of 1973 ( et seq.);16 U.S.C. 1531

children.label: B
children.text: a designation of critical habitat of the species under that Act; or

children.label: C
children.text: a recovery plan prepared for the species under that Act.

children.label: A
children.text: an area within or adjacent to an at-risk community that is identified in recommendations to the Secretary in a community wildfire protection plan; or

children.label: i
children.text: an area extending ½-mile from the boundary of an at-risk community;

children.label: I
children.text: has a sustained steep slope that creates the potential for wildfire behavior endangering the at-risk community;

children.label: II
children.text: has a geographic feature that aids in creating an effective fire break, such as a road or ridge top; or

children.label: III
children.text: is in condition class 3, as documented by the Secretary in the project-specific environmental analysis; and

children.label: iii
children.text: an area that is adjacent to an evacuation route for an at-risk community that the Secretary determines, in cooperation with the at-risk community, requires hazardous fuel reduction to provide safer evacuation from the at-risk community.
